# Alert Lifecycle

## Alert Types

Alerts are generated by the `CalculationService` after each recalculation, and by background jobs monitoring data freshness and shipping quote validity.

### Margin Alerts (generated by CalculationService)

| Alert Type | Severity | Trigger Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `BELOW_CRITICAL_MARGIN` | CRITICAL | `contributionMarginPercent < criticalThresholdPercent` |
| `BELOW_MINIMUM_MARGIN` | HIGH | `contributionMarginPercent < minimumMarginPercent` (and not critical) |
| `BELOW_WARNING_MARGIN` | WARNING | `contributionMarginPercent < warningThresholdPercent` (and not below min) |
| `NEGATIVE_PROFIT` | CRITICAL | `contributionProfit < 0` |

### Data Quality Alerts (generated by CalculationService)

| Alert Type | Severity | Trigger Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `MISSING_SELLING_PRICE` | HIGH | CustomerSku has no selling price |
| `MISSING_PRODUCT_COST` | HIGH | Product has no currentCost |
| `DIMENSION_FALLBACK_USED` | WARNING | dimensionSource = DUNNAGE_FALLBACK |

### Shipping Alerts (generated by background job)

| Alert Type | Severity | Trigger Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `NO_SHIPPING_QUOTE` | HIGH | CustomerSku needs a quote (PREPAID) but has none |
| `EXPIRED_QUOTE` | WARNING | All quotes for a PREPAID CustomerSku are past expiration |
| `QUOTE_EXPIRING_SOON` | INFO | Quote expires within 48 hours |

### Change Alerts (generated on data mutation)

| Alert Type | Severity | Trigger Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `COST_INCREASED` | WARNING | Product cost increased since last calculation |
| `COST_DECREASED` | INFO | Product cost decreased since last calculation |
| `PRICE_CHANGE` | INFO | CustomerSku selling price changed |
| `ALLOCATION_CHANGED` | WARNING | A CustomerAllocation affecting this SKU was modified |
| `MARGIN_REQUIREMENT_CHANGED` | INFO | CustomerMarginRequirement changed for this customer |

### Review Alerts

| Alert Type | Severity | Trigger Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `PENDING_REVIEW` | INFO | CustomerSku.reviewStatus set to PENDING |
| `REVIEW_OVERDUE` | WARNING | PENDING review not acted on within SLA window (configurable) |
| `ESCALATED` | HIGH | CustomerSku.reviewStatus set to ESCALATED |

---

## Severity Levels

| Severity | Color | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| CRITICAL | Red | Requires immediate action; profit may be negative |
| HIGH | Orange | Margin below minimum requirement |
| WARNING | Yellow | Margin below warning threshold or data quality issue |
| INFO | Blue | Informational; no action urgently required |

---

## Status Machine

Each `Alert` has a `status` field that transitions through:

```
OPEN → ACKNOWLEDGED → RESOLVED
  │                      ↑
  └──── SUPPRESSED ───────┘ (suppressed alerts can be resolved without acknowledging)
```

### Transitions

| From | To | Who | When |
|---|---|---|---|
| — | OPEN | System | Alert condition first detected |
| OPEN | ACKNOWLEDGED | Any assigned user | User clicks "Acknowledge" |
| OPEN | SUPPRESSED | Sales Manager or higher | User suppresses for a reason |
| ACKNOWLEDGED | RESOLVED | Any assigned user | Condition no longer applies after recalculation |
| SUPPRESSED | RESOLVED | System or user | Condition cleared |
| RESOLVED | OPEN | System | Condition recurs after a prior resolution |

### Auto-Resolution

When a recalculation produces `alertStatus = OK` for a CustomerSku:
- Any OPEN or ACKNOWLEDGED alerts of type `BELOW_CRITICAL_MARGIN`, `BELOW_MINIMUM_MARGIN`, `BELOW_WARNING_MARGIN`, `NEGATIVE_PROFIT` for that CustomerSku are auto-resolved.
- The `Alert.resolvedAt` is set to now(); `resolvedById` is null (system-resolved).
- An `AlertHistory` row is written.

Suppressed alerts are NOT auto-resolved; they require manual resolution.

---

## AlertHistory

Every status transition writes an `AlertHistory` row:

```json
{
  "alertId": "uuid",
  "fromStatus": "OPEN",
  "toStatus": "ACKNOWLEDGED",
  "changedById": "user-uuid",
  "note": "Pricing negotiation in progress",
  "createdAt": "ISO-8601"
}
```

This provides a full audit trail of who acted on each alert and when.

---

## Deduplication

Before creating a new `OPEN` alert:
1. Check if an OPEN alert of the same `alertType` and `customerSkuId` already exists.
2. If yes: do not create a duplicate. Optionally update the `triggeredAt` timestamp.
3. If the existing alert is RESOLVED or SUPPRESSED: create a new OPEN alert (the condition has recurred).

---

## Alert Dashboard Behavior

The role-aware dashboard shows:
- **CRITICAL alerts:** always visible to all assigned users.
- **HIGH alerts:** visible to Sales Managers and above.
- **WARNING / INFO:** visible to Pricing Analysts and above.

The ROI grid `alertStatus` column reflects the highest-severity OPEN alert for each CustomerSku, used to sort and filter the grid.

---

## Notification Integration

When an alert is created (status = OPEN) or escalated:
1. Find all users assigned to the CustomerSku's customer.
2. For each user with `view_alerts` permission:
   - Create an in-app `Notification` of type `ALERT`.
   - If the user has email notifications enabled for the alert severity: send email.

Notification preferences are per-user and per-severity:

| User Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| `notifyOnCritical` | BOOLEAN |
| `notifyOnHigh` | BOOLEAN |
| `notifyOnWarning` | BOOLEAN |
| `emailDigestFrequency` | IMMEDIATE / DAILY / WEEKLY / NEVER |
